questions.prompt: In a TCP receive stream, what does RCV.NXT identify?

choices.B: The first sequence position not yet received contiguously

questions.explanation: Acknowledgments describe the next contiguous byte expected. Out-of-order higher bytes do not advance RCV.NXT until the gap is filled.

questions.prompt: Which assumption is invalid for both TCP socket reads and incremental HTTP/HTML parsing?

choices.A: Input can be split at any byte boundary
choices.B: Parser state must survive between reads
choices.C: One read corresponds to one protocol message, header, or tag
choices.D: Resource limits must be checked while accumulating input

questions.explanation: TCP exposes an ordered byte stream without preserving application message boundaries. Every protocol delimiter and token may span reads or share one read with later data.

questions.prompt: Why is the course Telnet server restricted to loopback?

choices.A: Telnet is encrypted but too fast for real networks
choices.B: Telnet provides no secure confidentiality or authentication and the service exposes a command session

questions.explanation: A Telnet-style remote shell is intentionally insecure. Loopback containment reduces exposure but does not replace correct session isolation and cleanup.

questions.prompt: What should a dynamic loader do after applying relocations to an executable segment through a temporary writable mapping?

choices.A: Leave it writable and executable forever
choices.B: Apply final non-writable executable permissions before control transfer
choices.D: Copy the segment into the network stack

questions.explanation: Relocation may need a controlled write phase, but final permissions should enforce write xor execute. Bounds and relocation support are checked before writes.

questions.prompt: How should a text browser handle an ESC byte supplied by document content in plain-text output mode?

choices.A: Pass it directly to the terminal
choices.B: Escape, replace, or reject it so content cannot inject terminal control sequences
choices.C: Interpret it as HTML closing tag

questions.explanation: Terminal control bytes are an output trust boundary. Plain-text mode must make them inert rather than allowing a page to alter the terminal or deceive the learner.
